rulings.Case Title: SCOTT BRUSCELLA VS. BELAMY MILLER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 16, 2018, LINE 1 DEFENDANT, BELAMY MILLER'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel answers to form interrogatories (set one) is granted as follows: 1. Within two (2) days from the date of this order Defendant will provide Plaintiff with a copy of its form interrogatories with a check mark by each interrogatory to which Defendant is asking Plaintiff to provide responses. 2. Provided pursuant to paragraph (1) above, Plaintiff will serve full and complete responses, without objection, to each interrogatory marked by Defendant. 3. The Court will impose monetary sanctions against Plaintiff in the amount of $1,000.00 payment of which shall be made to Defendant within ten (10) days from the date of this order. Counsel for Defendant should prepare an appropriate order, present a copy to Plaintiff's counsel for approval as to form and deliver a copy of the propose order to the Court for signature at the hearing on November 16, 2018. Counsel are reminded that even though the tentative may be uncontested, someone must appear on November 16th with the proposed order for signature. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = PAR

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH MASSINGALE VS. CHARLES "CHUCK" RODRICK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike; Supporting Memorandum Of P And A; Declarations
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 16, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANTS CHARLES RODRICK, BRENT OESTERBLAD'S Joint Motion To Strike Defendants Brent Oesterblad and Charles Rodrick's anti-SLAPP motion to strike is GRANTED. (See CCP §425.16.) Plaintiff does not seriously dispute that his causes of action are ones "arising from protected activity." (See Navellier v. Sletten (2002) 29 Cal.4th 83, 88.) Further, plaintiff does not establish a probability that he will prevail on his claims. (CCP §425.16(b)(1).) For one example, plaintiff does not even try to rebut defendants' showings that his causes of action are barred by statutes of limitations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 16, 2018, LINE 2, Motion 1 DEFENDANTS, THOMAS WONG, C.K. KWAN, EDMOND HON, 633 PARTNERSHIP, and 633 HOLDINGS, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. rulings.Case Title: DON TUCKER ET AL VS. THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY OF CONNECTICUT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Renewed Request For Summary Adjudication On Issue I
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 16, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY OF CONNECTICUT'S Motion For Renewed Request For Summary Adjudication On Issue I Defendant The Travelers Indemnity Company of Connecticut's "motion for renewed request for summary adjudication on issue 1" is DENIED because it does not comply with the requirements of CCP §437c. Management, Inc. v. Mater Misericordiae Hospital (2008) 169 Cal.App.4th 357 merits extensive quotation: (1) "Motions for summary judgment may be brought only under Code of Civil Procedure section 437c and in accordance with its requirements;" (2) CCP §437c(f)(1) permits summary adjudication motions regarding "newly discovered facts or circumstances," but "[s]uch a motion is a "new summary judgment motion" that must comply with CCP §437c's requirements; and (3) CCP §1008 is not to the contrary; it "does not purport to authorize a new summary judgment motion that does not comply with the requirements for such motions set out in Code of Civil Procedure section 437c")(emphasis in original).) (Id. at 368.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Tucker is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD A. FRANKLIN VS. DONOVAN SF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant 1405 Franklin 13 Lp'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 16, 2018, Line 13, Defendant 1405 Franklin 13 Lp'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Defendant 1405 Franklin 13, LLC's motion to quash service of summons is GRANTED. The motion's hearing date was properly set. Plaintiff cites no authority for his notion that a communication from an insurance company effected service of summons. Plaintiff's "substitute service" on a UPS store's employee was ineffective - it was by an unregistered server at an address with no relation to defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for 1405 Franklin 13, LLC is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK A STATE CHARTERED BANK VS. RENKA PROP. LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Default And Leave To Defend
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 16, 2018, Line 11, Motion To Set Aside Default And Leave To Defend Defendant Renka Prop, LLC's motion to vacate entry of default and default judgment is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Renka seeks relief under Code of Civil Procedure §473(b) on the ground of attorney mistake. Yet, further mistakes plague Renka's motion papers. (1) The motion says it "is supported by declarations of both Renka's counsel," but only a declaration of attorney Paul Manasian was filed with the motion. (2) The Manasian declaration filed with Renka moving papers was an incomplete two-paragraph shell. (3) A new Manasian declaration filed (improperly) with Xelan's reply is itself incomplete; it ends in mid-sentence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Default And Leave To Defend
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 16, 2018, Line 12, Motion To Set Aside Default And Leave To Defend Defendant Xelan Prop 1, LLC's motion to vacate entry of default and default judgment is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Xelan seeks relief under Code of Civil Procedure §473(b) on the ground of attorney mistake. Yet, further mistakes plague Xelan's motion papers. (1) The motion says it "is supported by declarations of both Xelan's counsel," but only a declaration of attorney Paul Manasian was filed with the motion. (2) The Manasian declaration filed with Xelan's moving papers was an incomplete two-paragraph shell. (3) A new Manasian declaration filed (improperly) with Xelan's reply is itself incomplete; it ends in mid-sentence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: TIAN ZHANG VS. APEX CONSTRUCTION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 16, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT BOSCO LAI'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Bosco Lai's demurrers are OVERRULED. The first amended complaint adequately pleads alter ego liability against Lai. (See id. at 5-6.) The FAC also adequately alleges a connection between Apex Construction and Lai and the premises. (Id. at 28.) Rather than contradict the alter ego allegations as Lai argues, the FAC's exhibits illustrate lack of corporate formalities, thus bolstering those allegations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Zhang is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: NORTH BEACH CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION VS. GREGORY BRUBAKER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions [Ccp Sections 2030.290 Et Seq.]
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 16, 2018, LINE 4 PLAINTIFF, NORTH BEACH CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions [Ccp Sections 2030.290 Et Seq.] Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel further discovery responses from Defendant Gregory Brubaker is granted as follows: 1. Defendant will serve within ten (10) days code compliant responses to requests for admission Nos. 3 - 12. Defendant's objections are overruled. 2. Defendant will serve within fifteen (15) days full and complete responses, without objection, to special interrogatories Nos. 1 - 18. Defendant's objections are overruled. 3. Defendant will serve within fifteen (15) days full and complete responses without objection, to form interrogatories Nos. 2.8, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.6, 14.1, 14.2, 15.5, 50.1, 50.2, 50.3,50.4, 50,5, and 50.6. 4. Defendant will produce within fifteen (15) days from the date of this order without objections save for a claim of attorney-client privilege all documents responsive to Plaintiff's request for production of documents Nos. 1 - 9. To the extent that Plaintiff is withholding any documents on the grounds of attorney-client privilege, he will identify such documents with sufficient specificity to enable Plaintiff to challenge that claim. 5. Defendant is ordered to pay monetary sanctions in the amount of $3,1877.50 to Plaintiff within ten (10) days from the date of this order. Counsel for Plaintiff will prepare an appropriate order, submit a copy to Defendant for approval as to form and deliver a copy of the proposed order to the Court at the hearing on November 16, 2018. Counsel is reminded that even if the tentative ruling is not contested, counsel must deliver a proposed order to the Court on November 16, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = PAR

rulings.Case Title: JAMES PAUL GREEN VS. JOSH HOROWITZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 16, 2018, LINE 5 DEFENDANTS JOSH HOROWITZ and JENNIFER CHEN'S Motion To Compel Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: It appears that the deposition of Plaintiff has been taken after the filing of the motion to compel, rendering the motion moot. The only relief Defendant now seeks is the imposition of a monetary sanction against Plaintiff. After a review of all the papers the Court will impose no monetary sanctions. Counsel for the moving party will prepare an appropriate order, submit a copy to Plaintiff for approval as to form and deliver a copy to the Court at the hearing on November 16, 2018. Counsel is reminded that even though the tentative ruling may not be contested, counsel must present a proposed order to the Court at the hearing on November 16, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested = PAR
